description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The solar panels are elevated above a patterned platform, supported by angled metal struts that provide stability and clearance beneath the array. The platform below the panels displays a geometric, high-contrast design, possibly for aesthetic or functional demarcation. The structure appears suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, public parks, or roadside installations, offering both energy generation and potential shading. The frame is constructed from metallic materials, likely steel or aluminum, to ensure durability and resistance to environmental factors. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present, and the array is arranged in a single, continuous row.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a sturdy metal frame above a patterned platform, designed for urban energy generation and shading.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the supporting angled metal struts over the patterned platform.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the broad face of the solar panels and the symmetrical support structure beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the underside of the solar panels and the geometric platform design.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the solar panel array and the supporting frame, with the platform's pattern visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts and a rectangular frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 12 panels
